place: Akwesasne CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Akwesasne was founded in the mid‑18th century by Mohawk families from Kahnawake, a Catholic village south of Montreal.
The community’s name, Kana꞉takon, means “the village” in Mohawk.
In the 1940s, Ernest Benedict launched Akwesasne’s first newspaper, Kawehras!, which grew into the largest native newspaper worldwide.
The 1987 Akwesasne Task Force on the Environment formed to combat PCB contamination from nearby industries along the St. Lawrence River.
The Tribal Historic Preservation Office, located in the Ionkwakiohkwaró:ron building, protects Akwesasné’s cultural heritage under the NHPA and NAGPRA.
Akwesasne is one of several Kanienʼkehá꞉ka territories in present‑day Canada, alongside Kahnawake, Wahta, Tyendinaga, Kanesatake, and the Six Nations.
